title: Fly — Build fast. Run any code fearlessly.
description: Fly.io ships your apps close to users on a global Anycast network. Build fast, run any code fearlessly.

sections.title: Build fast. Run any code fearlessly.
sections.lede: Fly.io ships your apps close to users on a global Anycast network. The reference shape: a deploy platform where the deploy unit is a single binary + a single TOML, and the runtime is a hardware-virtualized Firecracker microVM.

background.alt: Editorial photograph illustrating distributed cloud infrastructure.

sections.text: Forge-static approximation of fly.io authored from SSR-extractable og:title + og:description (live ships a thin SSR with most content hydrated client-side). Pixel-rep rotation target per PlausiDen-Forge #222. Not affiliated with Fly.io; the substrate uses Fly as a reference shape for the editorial_purity_gate's edge-platform + Firecracker-MicroVM-deploy audit.

sections.body: Run any code fearlessly. A unit of deploy is a single binary + a single TOML; the runtime is a hardware-virtualized Firecracker microVM. The reference shape for editorial-density deploy platforms.
sections.attribution: fly.io public framing + PlausiDen-Forge substrate-doctrine reframe

sections.heading: Fly product surface.
sections.lede: Five primitives — Machines, Apps, Volumes, Anycast, Postgres — that compose into a deploy platform thinner than the runtime above it.

items.title: Machines
items.body: Firecracker microVMs as the unit of compute. Hardware virtualization, not container isolation. Sub-second start; ms-scale autoscale. Any code that runs on Linux runs on a Machine.

items.title: Apps
items.body: Multi-region apps from one fly.toml. Declarative deploy: regions, scale rules, health checks, secrets — all in one config the platform reads. Same shape regardless of stack.

items.title: Volumes
items.body: Per-Machine block storage. Persistent volumes attached to specific Machines. Lets stateful workloads (databases, queues, caches) run with the same deploy ergonomics as stateless apps.

items.title: Anycast
items.body: Global Anycast network. Same IP, routed to the closest region. TLS termination + WAF at the edge; app code never sees a 'choose a region' prompt at request time.

items.title: Postgres
items.body: Fly Postgres (managed). Multi-region Postgres deployed via fly.toml. Read replicas auto-routed via the same Anycast layer.

sections.text: Fly's substrate commits to a deploy unit (Machine) that's smaller than a container and harder-isolated than a VM. The reference shape: the deploy platform should be the thinnest layer above the runtime, not a multi-quarter migration target.
